title: Genista humilis
intro: Genista humilis, also known as dwarf broom or dwarf genista, is a low-growing, shrub-like legume native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and digestive issues. Genista humilis contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, including genistein, which has been shown to have anti-cancer and anti-inflammatory effects. The most common herbal preparations made from Genista humilis include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and support overall well-being.
taxonomy: Genista humilis, also known as dwarf broom or low broom, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). It is a small, shrubby plant that is native to Europe and Western Asia. As a member of the genus Genista, Genista humilis is closely related to other broom species. The classification of Genista humilis is further divided into the kingdom Plantae and the order Fabales. Genista humilis is classified under the family Fabaceae and the genus Genista.
uses: Genista humilis is a plant species known for its traditional medicinal uses, particularly in folk medicine. Its extracts and compounds have been studied for potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In some cultures, Genista humilis is used to treat digestive issues and skin problems. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use. Traditional remedies often involve infusions or decoctions made from the plant's leaves, stems, or roots.
benefits: Genista humilis is known for its potential health benefits, including its antioxidant properties and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, such as digestive issues and respiratory problems. Genista humilis is also rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. Research suggests that the plant may have antimicrobial and antiviral properties, making it a valuable resource for natural medicine. However, more studies are needed to fully understand the plant's potential health benefits and applications.

constituents: Genista humilis contains saponins and flavonoids as its active constituents, which are known for their medicinal properties. The saponins present in Genista humilis have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Flavonoids, on the other hand, are known for their antioxidant properties and ability to scavenge free radicals. Other studies have also identified coumarins, phenolic acids, and tannins in Genista humilis, contributing to its pharmacological activity. These compounds may be responsible for the plant's potential use in treating various health conditions.
parts: Genista humilis is known to have several medicinal parts, but the most notable ones are its leaves and flowers. The leaves and flowers of Genista humilis contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds may contribute to the plant's potential therapeutic uses, such as treating respiratory issues and infections. The leaves and flowers of Genista humilis are also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and digestive problems. preparations: Genista humilis is a shrub native to the Iberian Peninsula and parts of North Africa. However, specific information about herbal preparations from this plant is not readily available.
